user_query:
Please set up a small, local CI-like pipeline that fetches and ranks educational YouTube videos from a few official channels we watch with my child.

Inputs (use exactly these):
- input/channels.csv: CSV with two columns: handle,display_name. Use each handle to resolve its canonical YouTube channel ID by downloading the official handle page at the YouTube domain pattern '/@<handle>' and extracting the channel ID from the HTML.
- input/keywords.yaml: YAML listing categories, their weights, and keyword terms used to filter and score videos.

Requirements:
1) Discovery and extraction
   - For each handle in input/channels.csv, download the corresponding handle page HTML (YouTube domain pattern '/@<handle>') and save it to workspace/raw/handles/<handle>.html.
   - Extract the canonical channel ID from each handle page. Write all resolved IDs to output/channel_ids.csv with columns: handle,channel_id,display_name.
   - For each resolved channel_id, download the official public Atom feed on the YouTube domain pattern 'feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=<channel_id>' and save each raw feed to workspace/raw/feeds/<channel_id>.xml.

2) Build a structured dataset
   - Parse all feeds and produce a combined JSON array at output/videos_raw.json. Each entry must include at minimum: channel_handle, channel_id, video_id, title, published_at (ISO 8601), link (watch URL), and a derived thumbnail_url using the official YouTube thumbnail domain pattern 'i.ytimg.com/vi/<video_id>/hqdefault.jpg'.
   - Also download the watch page HTML for each kept video (top-ranked later; see step 3) from the YouTube domain pattern 'watch?v=<video_id>' and store it in workspace/pages/<video_id>.html. Include the relative path to this file in your final CSV for those videos.

3) Filter, sort, and rank
   - Using input/keywords.yaml, filter videos whose title or available description/summary contains at least one configured keyword term (case-insensitive). If a handle cannot be resolved or returns no feed entries, record a warning in output/summary.md and continue.
   - Compute a deterministic score for each filtered video that combines:
     a) Recency (higher score for more recent publish dates), and
     b) Keyword match strength (sum of weights for matched categories).
     Document your exact scoring formula in output/summary.md.
   - Sort all filtered videos by score descending and produce output/filtered_ranked.csv with columns: channel_handle,channel_id,video_id,title,published_at,score,matched_categories,thumbnail_path,watch_page_path,source_feed_path.

4) Download remote resources
   - For each filtered video, download its default thumbnail image (from the thumbnail domain pattern) and save to workspace/thumbnails/<video_id>.jpg. Reference this relative path in filtered_ranked.csv.

5) Report
   - Create output/summary.md containing: the list of handles processed, any resolution warnings, counts of total vs filtered videos per channel, a brief description of your ranking formula, and the top 10 ranked titles with their scores.

Notes:
- The pipeline must be runnable locally without credentials and regenerate all outputs deterministically from the current live pages/feeds.
- If fewer than 15 videos match across channels, include all available matches and note it in output/summary.md.
- All referenced output and workspace paths above are required artifacts.

Deliverables to produce:
- output/channel_ids.csv
- workspace/raw/handles/<handle>.html files
- workspace/raw/feeds/<channel_id>.xml files
- output/videos_raw.json
- output/filtered_ranked.csv
- workspace/thumbnails/<video_id>.jpg files for filtered videos
- workspace/pages/<video_id>.html files for at least the top 10 ranked videos
- output/summary.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
